event_description: Yes. Celadon Group, Inc. (a truckload trucking company) was engulfed in a significant accounting scandal. In 2017, the company disclosed accounting irregularities related to sale-leaseback and trade transactions of used trucks between Celadon's Quality Companies leasing subsidiary and a related party (Element Fleet Management, formerly GE affiliate). These transactions were used to inflate equipment values and hide losses, artificially boosting reported earnings. This led to: (1) delayed SEC filings and NYSE listing compliance issues, (2) a formal SEC investigation into the accounting practices, (3) a multi-year restatement of financial statements (fiscal years 2014-2016 and related periods), (4) departures of top finance/executive leadership including CEO Paul Will and CFO Bobby Peavler amid the scandal, (5) an Item 4.02 non-reliance disclosure regarding previously issued financial statements, and (6) ultimately delisting from the NYSE. The DOJ also pursued a criminal investigation, and Celadon reached a deferred prosecution agreement with a financial penalty around April 2019. The company subsequently filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and ceased trucking operations in December 2019.
approx_date: Accounting issues surfaced/restatement announced around April 2017; SEC/DOJ resolution around April 2019; bankruptcy filing December 2019
